title: dppca
text: dppca provides tools differentially private principal component analysis (PCA) visualization R. supports private PC direction estimation, private scree/PVE plots, private score plots, grouped score visualizations, interactive Shiny app.

title: dppca
text: can install development version GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("yejinjo0220/dppca")

title: dppca
text: main workflow : estimate private PC directions dp_pc_dir(). estimate plot private scree/PVE summaries dp_scree() dp_scree_plot(). compute plot private PCA score summaries dp_score() dp_score_plot(). optionally use grouped score visualizations Shiny app. examples use synthetic Gaussian cluster dataset included package.
code: library(dppca)  data(gau, package = "dppca") X <- gau

title: dppca
text: dp_pc_dir() estimates leading principal component directions differential privacy. returned object contains private principal component directions can used PCA summaries visualizations.
code: set.seed(123)  V <- dp_pc_dir(   X,   k = 5,   g_dppca = TRUE,   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-4 )  V

title: dppca
text: dp_scree() estimates private scree values proportions variance explained. method chosen method argument. package currently supports three scree estimation methods: "clipped": clipped mean based estimator; "pmwm": private modified winsorized mean based estimator; "huber": Huber-type robust estimator. Method-specific tuning parameters specified using control helper unctions clipped_control(), pmwm_control(), huber_control(). example, multiple scree methods can requested passing vector method named list control.
code: set.seed(123)  scree_clipped <- dp_scree(   X,   k = 5,   method = "clipped",   control = clipped_control(C_clip = 3),   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-4 )  scree_clipped set.seed(123)  scree_all <- dp_scree(   X,   k = 5,   method = c("clipped", "pmwm", "huber"),   control = list(     clipped = clipped_control(C_clip = 3),     pmwm = pmwm_control(a = 0, b = 50, trim_const = 10, eta = 0.01),     huber = huber_control(k_min_m2 = -10, k_max_m2 = 10, m2_frac = 1 / 4)   ),   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-4 )  scree_all

title: dppca
text: dp_scree_plot() visualizes private scree values private proportions variance explained.
code: set.seed(123)  scree_plot_all <- dp_scree_plot(   X,   k = 5,   method = c("clipped", "pmwm", "huber"),   control = list(     clipped = clipped_control(C_clip = 3),     pmwm = pmwm_control(a = 0, b = 50, trim_const = 10, eta = 0.01),     huber = huber_control(k_min_m2 = -10, k_max_m2 = 10, m2_frac = 1 / 4)   ),   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-4 ) scree_plot_all

title: dppca
text: dp_score() computes differentially private summaries two-dimensional PCA scores using histogram-based methods. Available score methods include: "add": additive histogram method; "sparse": sparse histogram method. Use method = "add" method = "sparse" run one histogram method, method = c("add", "sparse") compute .
code: set.seed(123)  score_result <- dp_score(   X,   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-4,   bins = c(8, 8),   method = "add" )  score_result

title: dppca
text: dp_score_plot() draws private score plots based histogram summaries returned dp_score(). method omitted, additive sparse histogram methods used.
code: set.seed(123)  score_plot <- dp_score_plot(   X,   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-4,   bins = c(15, 15) )  score_plot$plot$all

title: dppca
text: data group labels, dp_score_group() dp_score_plot_group() provide grouped versions private score. Compute grouped private score. Draw grouped private score plot.
code: data(gau_g, package = "dppca") X_g <- gau_g set.seed(123) score_group <- dp_score_group(   X_g,   group = "group",   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-4,   bins = c(8, 8),   method = "add" )  score_group set.seed(123)  score_group_plot <- dp_score_plot_group(   X_g,   group = "group",   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-4,   bins = c(15, 15), )  score_group_plot$plot$all

title: Differentially private score histograms ‚ÄĒ dp_score
text: Let \(v_a\) \(v_b\) principal component directions selected axes = c(, b) \(1 \le < b \le ncol(X)\). preprocessing, score point \(\)th observation \(s_i = (x_i^\top v_a, x_i^\top v_b)\). non-private score plot display points \(s_1, \ldots, s_n\) directly. function instead summarizes empirical distribution two-dimensional histogram releases private versions histogram visualization. plotting frame constructed privately score coordinates. frame center estimated coordinate-wise private medians, frame radius estimated private 0.99 quantile Euclidean distances private center. resulting private radius inflated fixed factor used form square plotting frame. private frame computed using smooth-sensitivity based quantile mechanism (Nissim et al. 2007) . private histogram computed rectangular grid defined private frame bin counts bins. row-level adjacency, changing one observation can increase one bin count one decrease another one, giving \(\ell_1\) sensitivity \(2\) \(\ell_2\) sensitivity \(\sqrt{2}\) count vector. Two private histogram mechanisms supported: "add" constructs additive differentially private histogram adding Gaussian noise bin counts, clipping negative noisy counts zero, normalizing result. additive-noise approach commonly used private histograms; see Wasserman Zhou (2010) . "sparse" constructs sparse differentially private histogram settings many bins empty. perturbs nonzero empirical bin proportions keeps bins whose noisy values exceed stability threshold, following stability-based private histogram idea Karwa Vadhan (2017) . privacy parameters allocated across privacy-consuming steps. g_dppca = FALSE, half eps delta used private frame construction half private histogram. g_dppca = TRUE, parameters split equally among private direction estimation, private frame construction, private histogram release. detailed procedure mathematical formulations, refer https://yejinjo0220.github.io/dppca/articles/dp_score.

title: Differentially private scree values ‚ÄĒ dp_scree
text: Let \(X\) denote preprocessed data matrix let \(v_l\) \(l\)th principal component direction. \(l\)th score vector \(z_l = X v_l\). corresponding sample scree value can written $$   \hat{\lambda}_l   = v_l^\top \widehat{\Sigma} v_l   = \frac{1}{n - 1}\sum_{= 1}^n z_{il}^2   = \frac{n}{n - 1}\left(\frac{1}{n}\sum_{= 1}^n w_{il}\right),   \qquad w_{il} = z_{il}^2. $$ Therefore, scree value estimated privately estimating mean \(w_{1l}, \ldots, w_{nl}\) multiplying \(n/(n - 1)\). supported methods differ private mean estimated: "clipped" clips squared scores \(w_{\ell}\) C_clip applies Gaussian mechanism (Dwork Roth 2014) . simplest option depends directly clipping threshold. "pmwm" uses private modified winsorized mean approach Ramsay Spicker (2025) , adapted accompanying Python implementation R. privately estimates tail cutoffs, winsorizes squared scores \(w_{\ell}\), releases noisy winsorized mean. "huber" uses Huber-type private robust mean estimator based noisy gradient descent, following Yu et al. (2024) . argument g_dppca controls principal component directions obtained. g_dppca = FALSE, directions computed non-privately eigenvector sample covariance, full privacy parameters eps delta used private scree value estimation. g_dppca = TRUE, directions computed privately using dp_pc_dir(). case, privacy parameters split equally: dp_pc_dir() receives eps = eps / 2 delta = delta / 2 private direction estimation, remaining eps / 2 delta / 2 used private scree value estimation. mono = TRUE, final monotone adjustment post-processing step change privacy guarantee. detailed procedure mathematical formulations, refer https://yejinjo0220.github.io/dppca/articles/dp_scree.
